_schema: proper_nouns v1. Key = unmacronized lemma. The graded gate flag-and-WARNs (never rejects) on these proper nouns per the graded-gate spec — proper nouns are seeded from a standard second-year Latin vocabulary index (Caesar, Gaul, Cicero, etc.). Where the macronized citation is known it is stored + applied; where unknown the gate warns without macron correction. Expansion is a documented later phase.

Germani.note: long ā (the people, plural nom); 2nd decl m, Germānī / Germānōrum

Graecia.note: ae diphthong (short), short i; 1st decl, Graeciae

Hannibal.note: short a throughout; 3rd decl m, Hannibalis

Cicero.note: short i, long ō (1sg-style nom); 3rd decl m, Cicerōnis

Martialis.note: long ā (from Mars); 3rd decl m, Mārtiālis

Vergilius.note: short e; 2nd decl m, Vergiliī (Publius Vergilius Maro)

Helvetii.note: short e, long ī (plural nom); 2nd decl m, Helvetiī / Helvetiōrum

Iulia.note: long ū (gens Iulia); 1st decl f, Iūliae — FR cast (Iulius's daughter)

Iulius.note: long ū (gens Iulia); 2nd decl m, Iūliī — FR patriarch (Iulius pater)

Aemilia.note: ae diphthong (short), short i; 1st decl f, Aemiliae — FR cast (Iulius's wife)

Marcus.note: long ā (from Mars); 2nd decl m, Mārcī — FR cast (Mārcus, son)

Quintus.note: long ī; 2nd decl m, Quīntī — FR cast (Quintus, son)

Sylla.note: short y; 1st decl, Syllae — FR cast (the dictator, Sylla/Sulla spelling)
